```erlang
%% Helper function to check for primality
%% Based on the optimized check from the Javascript reference.
is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 or N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    do
        I =:= 5,
        check_loop(N, I)
    after
        check_loop(N, I) ->
            true;
        check_loop(N, I) ->
            false
    end.

check_loop(N, I) when I * I > N ->
    true;
check_loop(N, I) ->
    case (N rem I, N rem (I + 2)) of
        {0, _} ->
            false;
        {_, 0} ->
            false;
        _ ->
            check_loop(N, I + 6)
    end.

%% Helper function to find the next prime number >= N
find_next_prime(N) ->
    do
        case is_prime(N) of
            true -> N;
            false -> find_next_prime(N + 1)
        end
    after
        find_next_prime(N)
    end.

%% Helper function to find the next non-prime number >= N
find_next_non_prime(N) ->
    do
        case is_prime(N) of
            false -> N;
            true -> find_next_non_prime(N + 1)
        end
    after
        find_next_non_prime(N)
    end.

solve(Nums) ->
    TotalOperations = 0,
    L = length(Nums),
    
    Fun = fun(I, CurrentNum, Acc) ->
        case I rem 2 of
            0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
                case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                    true -> 
                        fun(_, _, Acc) -> Acc end;
                    false ->
                        NextPrime = find_next_prime(CurrentNum),
                        Ops = NextPrime - CurrentNum,
                        fun(I+1, _, NewAcc) -> NewAcc + Ops end
                end;
            1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
                case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                    false -> 
                        fun(_, _, Acc) -> Acc end;
                    true ->
                        NextNonPrime = find_next_non_prime(CurrentNum),
                        Ops = NextNonPrime - CurrentNum,
                        fun(I+1, _, NewAcc) -> NewAcc + Ops end
                end
        end
    end,

    % Use a recursive helper to iterate and sum up operations
    sum_ops(0, Nums, 0).

sum_ops(I, [], Total) ->
    Total;
sum_ops(I, [CurrentNum|Rest], Total) ->
    case I rem 2 of
        0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
            case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                true ->
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total);
                false ->
                    NextPrime = find_next_prime(CurrentNum),
                    Ops = NextPrime - CurrentNum,
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total + Ops)
            end;
        1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
            case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                false ->
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total);
                true ->
                    NextNonPrime = find_next_non_prime(CurrentNum),
                    Ops = NextNonPrime - CurrentNum,
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total + Ops)
            end
    end.
```

is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 or N rem 3 == 0 ->

is_prime(N) ->

        I =:= 5,
        check_loop(N, I)
    after
        check_loop(N, I) ->
            true;
        check_loop(N, I) ->
            false
    end.

check_loop(N, I) when I * I > N ->
    true;
check_loop(N, I) ->
    case (N rem I, N rem (I + 2)) of
        {0, _} ->
            false;
        {_, 0} ->
            false;
        _ ->
            check_loop(N, I + 6)
    end.

%% Helper function to find the next prime number >= N
find_next_prime(N) ->
    do
        case is_prime(N) of
            true -> N;
            false -> find_next_prime(N + 1)
        end
    after
        find_next_prime(N)
    end.

%% Helper function to find the next non-prime number >= N
find_next_non_prime(N) ->
    do
        case is_prime(N) of
            false -> N;
            true -> find_next_non_prime(N + 1)
        end
    after
        find_next_non_prime(N)
    end.

solve(Nums) ->
    TotalOperations = 0,
    L = length(Nums),
    
    Fun = fun(I, CurrentNum, Acc) ->
        case I rem 2 of
            0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
                case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                    true -> 
                        fun(_, _, Acc) -> Acc end;
                    false ->
                        NextPrime = find_next_prime(CurrentNum),
                        Ops = NextPrime - CurrentNum,
                        fun(I+1, _, NewAcc) -> NewAcc + Ops end
                end;
            1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
                case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                    false -> 
                        fun(_, _, Acc) -> Acc end;
                    true ->
                        NextNonPrime = find_next_non_prime(CurrentNum),
                        Ops = NextNonPrime - CurrentNum,
                        fun(I+1, _, NewAcc) -> NewAcc + Ops end
                end
        end
    end,

    % Use a recursive helper to iterate and sum up operations
    sum_ops(0, Nums, 0).

sum_ops(I, [], Total) ->
    Total;
sum_ops(I, [CurrentNum|Rest], Total) ->
    case I rem 2 of
        0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
            case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                true ->
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total);
                false ->
                    NextPrime = find_next_prime(CurrentNum),
                    Ops = NextPrime - CurrentNum,
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total + Ops)
            end;
        1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
            case is_prime(CurrentNum) of
                false ->
                    sum_ops(I+1, Rest, Total);

Please generate code to solve the following task:

给你一个整数数组 `nums`。
如果满足以下条件，则认为数组是**交替质数**数组：
 -**偶数**下标（从 0 开始）处的元素是**质数**。
 -**奇数**下标处的元素是**非质数**。

在一次操作中，你可以将任何元素**增加**1。
返回将 `nums` 转换为**交替质数**数组所需的**最少**操作次数。
**质数**是指大于 1 且仅有两个因数（1 和其本身）的自然数。

**示例 1：**
> 
**输入：**`nums = [1,2,3,4]`
**输出：**`3`
**解释：**
 - 下标 0 处的元素必须是质数。将 `nums[0] = 1` 增加到 2，使用 1 次操作。
 - 下标 1 处的元素必须是非质数。将 `nums[1] = 2` 增加到 4，使用 2 次操作。
 - 下标 2 处的元素已经是质数。
 - 下标 3 处的元素已经是非质数。

总操作次数 = `1 + 2 = 3`。
**示例 2：**
> 
**输入：**`nums = [5,6,7,8]`
**输出：**`0`
**解释：**
 - 下标 0 和 2 处的元素已经是质数。
 - 下标 1 和 3 处的元素已经是非质数。

不需要任何操作。
**示例 3：**
> 
**输入：**`nums = [4,4]`
**输出：**`1`
**解释：**
 - 下标 0 处的元素必须是质数。将 `nums[0] = 4` 增加到 5，使用 1 次操作。
 - 下标 1 处的元素已经是非质数。

总操作次数 = 1。

**提示：**
 - `1 <= nums.length <= 10^5`
 - `1 <= nums[i] <= 10^5`

__data__.output:
```erlang
-module(solver).
-export([solve/1]).

%% Helper function to check if a number is prime
%% Based on the constraints (nums[i] <= 10^5), a simple trial division is sufficient.
is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 orelse N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    fun is_composite(I) when I * I > N ->
        true;
    is_composite(I) when N rem I == 0 orelse N rem (I + 2) == 0 ->
        false;
    is_composite(I) ->
        is_composite(I + 6)
    end(5).

%% Helper function to find the smallest number >= N that satisfies a predicate P
%% P is a function: P(number) -> boolean
find_next_match(N, P) ->
    case P(N) of
        true -> N;
        false -> 
            case find_next_match(N + 1, P) of
                M -> M
            end
    end.

%% Main solving function
solve(nums) ->
    lists:foldl(calculate_cost, 0, nums, 0).

%% Accumulator function for foldl
%% Acc: total_operations_so_far
%% CurrentNum: nums[i]
%% Index: i
calculate_cost(Acc, CurrentNum, i) ->
    case i rem 2 of
        0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
            if is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                Acc; % Already prime, no cost
            else
                NextPrime = find_next_match(CurrentNum, fun(N) -> is_prime(N) end),
                Cost = NextPrime - CurrentNum,
                Acc + Cost
            end;
        1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
            if not is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                Acc; % Already non-prime, no cost
            else
                % Find smallest N >= CurrentNum + 1 such that is_prime(N) is false
                NextNonPrime = find_next_match(CurrentNum + 1, fun(N) -> not is_prime(N) end),
                Cost = NextNonPrime - CurrentNum,
                Acc + Cost
            end
    end.
```

```erlang
-module(solver).
-export([solve/1]).

%% Helper function to check if a number is prime
%% Based on the constraints (nums[i] <= 10^5), a simple trial division is sufficient.
is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 orelse N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    fun is_composite(I) when I * I > N ->
        true;
    is_composite(I) when N rem I == 0 orelse N rem (I + 2) == 0 ->
        false;
    is_composite(I) ->
        is_composite(I + 6)
    end(5).

%% Helper function to find the smallest number >= N that satisfies a predicate P
%% P is a function: P(number) -> boolean
find_next_match(N, P) ->
    case P(N) of
        true -> N;
        false -> 
            case find_next_match(N + 1, P) of
                M -> M
            end
    end.

%% Main solving function
solve(nums) ->
    lists:foldl(calculate_cost, 0, nums, 0).

%% Accumulator function for foldl
%% Acc: total_operations_so_far
%% CurrentNum: nums[i]
%% Index: i
calculate_cost(Acc, CurrentNum, i) ->
    case i rem 2 of
        0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
            if is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                Acc; % Already prime, no cost
            else
                NextPrime = find_next_match(CurrentNum, fun(N) -> is_prime(N) end),
                Cost = NextPrime - CurrentNum,
                Acc + Cost
            end;
        1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
            if not is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                Acc; % Already non-prime, no cost
            else
                % Find smallest N >= CurrentNum + 1 such that is_prime(N) is false
                NextNonPrime = find_next_match(CurrentNum + 1, fun(N) -> not is_prime(N) end),
                Cost = NextNonPrime - CurrentNum,
                Acc + Cost
            end
    end.
```

```erlang
%%% Helper functions for primality testing %%%

% is_prime/1: Checks if a number N is prime.
is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 orelse N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    search_prime(N, 5).

% search_prime/2: Optimized check using 6k +/- 1 method.
search_prime(N, I) when I * I > N ->
    true;
search_prime(N, I) when N rem I == 0 orelse N rem (I+2) == 0 ->
    false;
search_prime(N, I) ->
    search_prime(N, I+6).

% is_non_prime/1: Checks if a number N is non-prime (i.e., not prime).
% This aligns with the JS implementation where !isPrime(n) is used.
is_non_prime(N) ->
    \+ is_prime(N).

%%% Main solver function %%%

solve(Nums) ->
    TotalOperations = sum(lists:map(fun(I, N) -> calculate_ops(I, N) end, lists:enumerate(Nums))),
    TotalOperations.

% calculate_ops/2: Calculates the minimum operations needed for a specific element.
calculate_ops(I, N) when I rem 2 == 0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
    case find_next_prime(N) of
        {Ops, _} -> Ops;
        _ -> 0 % Should not happen given constraints
    end;
calculate_ops(I, N) when I rem 2 /= 0 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
    case find_next_non_prime(N) of
        {Ops, _} -> Ops;
        _ -> 0 % Should not happen given constraints
    end.

% find_next_prime/1: Finds the smallest number >= N that is prime, and returns the cost.
find_next_prime(N) ->
    SearchN = N,
    do_search(SearchN, N).

do_search(CurrentN, StartN) when is_prime(CurrentN) ->
    {CurrentN - StartN, CurrentN};
do_search(CurrentN, StartN) ->
    do_search(CurrentN + 1, StartN).

% find_next_non_prime/1: Finds the smallest number >= N that is non-prime, and returns the cost.
find_next_non_prime(N) ->
    SearchN = N,
    do_search_np(SearchN, N).

do_search_np(CurrentN, StartN) when is_non_prime(CurrentN) ->
    {CurrentN - StartN, CurrentN};
do_search_np(CurrentN, StartN) ->
    do_search_np(CurrentN + 1, StartN).
```

__data__.content:
```erlang
%%% Helper functions for primality testing %%%

% is_prime/1: Checks if a number N is prime.
is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 orelse N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    search_prime(N, 5).

% search_prime/2: Optimized check using 6k +/- 1 method.
search_prime(N, I) when I * I > N ->
    true;
search_prime(N, I) when N rem I == 0 orelse N rem (I+2) == 0 ->
    false;
search_prime(N, I) ->
    search_prime(N, I+6).

% is_non_prime/1: Checks if a number N is non-prime (i.e., not prime).
% This aligns with the JS implementation where !isPrime(n) is used.
is_non_prime(N) ->
    \+ is_prime(N).

%%% Main solver function %%%

solve(Nums) ->
    TotalOperations = sum(lists:map(fun(I, N) -> calculate_ops(I, N) end, lists:enumerate(Nums))),
    TotalOperations.

% calculate_ops/2: Calculates the minimum operations needed for a specific element.
calculate_ops(I, N) when I rem 2 == 0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
    case find_next_prime(N) of
        {Ops, _} -> Ops;
        _ -> 0 % Should not happen given constraints
    end;
calculate_ops(I, N) when I rem 2 /= 0 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
    case find_next_non_prime(N) of
        {Ops, _} -> Ops;
        _ -> 0 % Should not happen given constraints
    end.

% find_next_prime/1: Finds the smallest number >= N that is prime, and returns the cost.
find_next_prime(N) ->
    SearchN = N,
    do_search(SearchN, N).

do_search(CurrentN, StartN) when is_prime(CurrentN) ->
    {CurrentN - StartN, CurrentN};
do_search(CurrentN, StartN) ->
    do_search(CurrentN + 1, StartN).

% find_next_non_prime/1: Finds the smallest number >= N that is non-prime, and returns the cost.
find_next_non_prime(N) ->
    SearchN = N,
    do_search_np(SearchN, N).

do_search_np(CurrentN, StartN) when is_non_prime(CurrentN) ->
    {CurrentN - StartN, CurrentN};
do_search_np(CurrentN, StartN) ->
    do_search_np(CurrentN + 1, StartN).
```

__data__.output:
```erlang
-module(solution_module).

%% Helper function to check for primality
%% Based on the complexity requirements and constraints (up to 10^5 + small increment),
%% a standard trial division method is sufficient.

is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 or N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    loop(5, N).

loop(I, N) when I * I > N ->
    true;
loop(I, N) ->
    case N rem I of
        0 -> false;
        _ ->
            case N rem (I + 2) of
                0 -> false;
                _ -> loop(I + 6, N)
            end
    end.

%% Helper function to find the next prime >= N
find_next_prime(N) ->
    loop_p(N).

loop_p(N) when is_prime(N) ->
    N;
loop_p(N) ->
    loop_p(N + 1).

%% Helper function to find the next non-prime >= N
find_next_non_prime(N) ->
    loop_np(N).

loop_np(N) when \+ is_prime(N) ->
    N;
loop_np(N) ->
    loop_np(N + 1).


solve(Nums) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, CurrentNum, Index) ->
        case Index rem 2 of
            0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
                if is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                    Acc; % Already prime, no cost
                else
                    NextPrime = find_next_prime(CurrentNum + 1),
                    NewAcc = Acc + (NextPrime - CurrentNum)
                end;
            1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
                if \+ is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                    Acc; % Already non-prime, no cost
                else
                    NextNonPrime = find_next_non_prime(CurrentNum + 1),
                    NewAcc = Acc + (NextNonPrime - CurrentNum)
                end
        end
    end, 0, Nums, 0).
```

-module(solution_module).

%% Helper function to check for primality
%% Based on the complexity requirements and constraints (up to 10^5 + small increment),
%% a standard trial division method is sufficient.

is_prime(N) when N =< 1 ->
    false;
is_prime(2) ->
    true;
is_prime(3) ->
    true;
is_prime(N) when N rem 2 == 0 or N rem 3 == 0 ->
    false;
is_prime(N) ->
    loop(5, N).

loop(I, N) when I * I > N ->
    true;
loop(I, N) ->
    case N rem I of
        0 -> false;
        _ ->
            case N rem (I + 2) of
                0 -> false;
                _ -> loop(I + 6, N)
            end
    end.

%% Helper function to find the next prime >= N
find_next_prime(N) ->
    loop_p(N).

loop_p(N) when is_prime(N) ->
    N;
loop_p(N) ->
    loop_p(N + 1).

%% Helper function to find the next non-prime >= N
find_next_non_prime(N) ->
    loop_np(N).

loop_np(N) when \+ is_prime(N) ->
    N;
loop_np(N) ->
    loop_np(N + 1).


solve(Nums) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, CurrentNum, Index) ->
        case Index rem 2 of
            0 -> % Even index: Must be prime
                if is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                    Acc; % Already prime, no cost
                else
                    NextPrime = find_next_prime(CurrentNum + 1),
                    NewAcc = Acc + (NextPrime - CurrentNum)
                end;
            1 -> % Odd index: Must be non-prime
                if \+ is_prime(CurrentNum) ->
                    Acc; % Already non-prime, no cost
                else
                    NextNonPrime = find_next_non_prime(CurrentNum + 1),
                    NewAcc = Acc + (NextNonPrime - CurrentNum)
                end
        end
    end, 0, Nums, 0).

__data__.output:
```erlang
-module(solution_module).

%% Helper function to check if a number is prime
% Based on standard primality testing up to sqrt(n)
isPrime(N) when N < 2 -> false;
isPrime(N) when N =:= 2 -> true;
isPrime(N) when N mod 2 =:= 0 -> false;
isPrime(N) ->
    % Check divisibility by numbers of the form 6k +/- 1
    check_prime(N, 3).

check_prime(N, I) when I * I > N -> true;
check_prime(N, I) when N mod I =:= 0 -> false;
check_prime(N, I) when N mod ((I + 2)) =:= 0 -> false;
check_prime(N, I) -> check_prime(N, I + 6).

%% Helper function to find the minimum operations needed for an element
%% index is 0-based
find_min_ops(Num, Index) ->
    if Index mod 2 =:= 0 ->
        % Even index: Must be prime
        min_ops_to_prime(Num)
    ;
        % Odd index: Must be non-prime
        min_ops_to_non_prime(Num)
    end.

%% Find smallest non-negative difference to make Num prime
min_ops_to_prime(Num) ->
    if isPrime(Num) -> 0;
    true ->
        % Search upwards for the next prime
        find_next_prime(Num, 1).

find_next_prime(N, Diff) ->
    NewNum = N + Diff,
    if isPrime(NewNum) -> Diff;
    true ->
        find_next_prime(N, Diff + 1)
    end.

%% Find smallest non-negative difference to make Num non-prime
min_ops_to_non_prime(Num) ->
    if not isPrime(Num) -> 0;
    true ->
        % Search upwards for the next non-prime number
        find_next_non_prime(Num, 1).

find_next_non_prime(N, Diff) ->
    NewNum = N + Diff,
    if not isPrime(NewNum) -> Diff;
    true ->
        find_next_non_prime(N, Diff + 1)
    end.

%% Main solver function
solve(Nums) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, N_i, I) ->
        Ops = find_min_ops(N_i, I),
        Acc + Ops
    end, 0, Nums, 0).
```

__data__.content:
```erlang
-module(solution_module).

%% Helper function to check if a number is prime
% Based on standard primality testing up to sqrt(n)
isPrime(N) when N < 2 -> false;
isPrime(N) when N =:= 2 -> true;
isPrime(N) when N mod 2 =:= 0 -> false;
isPrime(N) ->
    % Check divisibility by numbers of the form 6k +/- 1
    check_prime(N, 3).

check_prime(N, I) when I * I > N -> true;
check_prime(N, I) when N mod I =:= 0 -> false;
check_prime(N, I) when N mod ((I + 2)) =:= 0 -> false;
check_prime(N, I) -> check_prime(N, I + 6).

%% Helper function to find the minimum operations needed for an element
%% index is 0-based
find_min_ops(Num, Index) ->
    if Index mod 2 =:= 0 ->
        % Even index: Must be prime
        min_ops_to_prime(Num)
    ;
        % Odd index: Must be non-prime
        min_ops_to_non_prime(Num)
    end.

%% Find smallest non-negative difference to make Num prime
min_ops_to_prime(Num) ->
    if isPrime(Num) -> 0;
    true ->
        % Search upwards for the next prime
        find_next_prime(Num, 1).

find_next_prime(N, Diff) ->
    NewNum = N + Diff,
    if isPrime(NewNum) -> Diff;
    true ->
        find_next_prime(N, Diff + 1)
    end.

%% Find smallest non-negative difference to make Num non-prime
min_ops_to_non_prime(Num) ->
    if not isPrime(Num) -> 0;
    true ->
        % Search upwards for the next non-prime number
        find_next_non_prime(Num, 1).

find_next_non_prime(N, Diff) ->
    NewNum = N + Diff,
    if not isPrime(NewNum) -> Diff;
    true ->
        find_next_non_prime(N, Diff + 1)
    end.

%% Main solver function
solve(Nums) ->
    lists:foldl(fun(Acc, N_i, I) ->
        Ops = find_min_ops(N_i, I),
        Acc + Ops
    end, 0, Nums, 0).
```
